zh.Adlington, Cheshire, Inglaterra: 英國英格蘭爾阿德林頓
zh.Knutsford, Cheshire, England: 英國英格蘭爾可努恰福德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Catherine de Leigh於1314年出生在英國英格蘭爾阿德林頓時，她的父親John de Legh Venables I35歲，母親Elena de Baguley34歲。她於1325年之前與Roger Le Bruen結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於從1352年到1406年在英國。

zh-hans.Adlington, Cheshire, Inglaterra: 英国英格兰爾阿德林頓

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Catherine de Leigh于1314年出生在英国英格兰爾阿德林頓时，她的父亲John de Legh Venables I35岁，母亲Elena de Baguley34岁。她于1325年之前与Roger Le Bruen结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于从1352年到1406年在英国。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Leigh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places (in at least sixteen counties, but especially Leigh in Lancashire) named either with the nominative case of Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’ (see Lee ) or with lēage, a late dative form of this word (see Lye ).
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 李, based on its Cantonese pronunciation. See   Li   1.

variants.name: Leah
meanings: English: variant of   Leigh  .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 李 and 利, see   Li   1 and 3.

variants.name: Lee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sang, Jae, Jong, Jung, Sung, Yong, Kyung, Seung, Dong, Kwang, Myung.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a meadow or a patch of arable land, Middle English lee, lea, from Old English lēa, dative case (used after a preposition) of lēah, which originally meant ‘wood or glade’.

English: habitational name from any of the many places in England named with Old English lēah ‘wood, glade’, including Lee in Buckinghamshire, Essex, Hampshire, Kent, and Shropshire, and Lea in Cheshire, Derbyshire, Herefordshire, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, and Wiltshire.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Catherine de Leigh was born in 1314, in Adlington, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, John de Legh Venables I, was 35 and her mother, Elena de Baguley, was 34. She married Roger Le Bruen before 1325. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died from 1352 to 1406, in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Catherine de Leigh est née en 1314, à Adlington, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John de Legh Venables I, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Elena de Baguley, avait 34 ans. Elle a épousé Roger Le Bruen avant 1325. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée du 1352 au 1406, à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Catherine de Leigh 1314 in Adlington, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John de Legh Venables I 35 und ihre Mutter Elena de Baguley 34. Sie hat vor 1325 Roger Le Bruen geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb von 1352 bis 1406 in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Catherine de Leigh è nata il nel 1314 nel Adlington, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, John de Legh Venables I, aveva 35 anni e sua madre, Elena de Baguley, aveva 34 anni. Lei sposato Roger Le Bruen prima del 1325. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì da 1352 a 1406, nel England.

ja.lifeSummary: Catherine de Leighが1314年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアアドリントンで生まれた時，父John de Legh Venables Iは35歳，母Elena de Baguleyは34歳であった。1325年以前にRoger Le Bruenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女はから1352年まで1406年にイギリスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Catherine de Leigh(이)가 1314년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 애들링턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Legh Venables I(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elena de Baguley(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 1325년 이전 Roger Le Bruen(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1352년 부터 1406년 까지 영국에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Catherine de Leigh nasceu em 1314, em Adlington, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John de Legh Venables I, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Elena de Baguley, tinha 34 anos. Ela casou-se com Roger Le Bruen antes 1325. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu de 1352 a 1406, em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Catherine de Leigh родилась в 1314 г. в местности адлингтон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John de Legh Venables I было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Elena de Baguley, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Roger Le Bruen перед 1325 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла с 1352 по 1406 в местности Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Catherine de Leigh nació en 1314, en Adlington, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John de Legh Venables I, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Elena de Baguley, tenía 34 años. Se casó con Roger Le Bruen antes de 1325. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió desde 1352 hasta 1406, en Inglaterra.
